history.content: Implemented and completed ✅ `prop_1774651223493_e51607` exactly as requested. ### What changed Edited **`src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`** to add end-to-end inbound Telegram image handling: - Extended Telegram message typing to include: - `caption` - `photo[]` - `document` - Added helper methods: - `isImageDocument(...)` - `getTelegramFilePath(fileId)` - `downloadTelegramFileAsDataUrl(filePath)` - Updated poll routing to process **any** `update.message` (not only text). - Refactored inbound normalization so handler supports: - text - caption - photo-only - image document - Preserved existing command behavior (`/start`, `/commands`, `/browse`, etc.) as text-triggered. - Added image context injection into `handleChat` caller context with deterministic fields: - `INBOUND_TELEGRAM_IMAGE: true` - `source` (`photo` / `document`) - `mime_type` - `size_bytes` - `data_url` - `caption_text` (if present) - Preserved session/history persistence for media messages by storing: - caption text when present - otherwise synthetic placeholder (`[Telegram image received: ...]`) - Added graceful failure handling for Telegram media fetch/download errors with user-facing message and no poll-loop crash. - Removed one duplicated unauthorized allowlist block discovered during edit cleanup. ### Build verification Ran mandatory build check: - `npm run build` ✅ - TypeScript compile passed (`tsc` exit 0) ### Acceptance checklist outcome - Photo + caption path: implemented - Photo-only path: implemented - Image document path: implemented - Text command non-regression: preserved in logic - Media fetch failure path: implemented with non-fatal handling - Build/type safety: passed No additional files were created or deleted.
